title: Causal LM Training Step (numpy)
description:
Implement one training step of a Causal Language Model (CLM) using numpy.

In CLM (GPT-style):
- Input: tokens[0..T-1]
- Targets: tokens[1..T] (next-token prediction)
- The model predicts the next token at each position autoregressively

**Forward pass:**
1. Embedding lookup: input_ids → X, shape (T-1, embed_dim)
2. Linear projection: X → logits, shape (T-1, vocab_size)
3. Cross-entropy loss against shifted targets

Return the mean cross-entropy loss.
constraints: Input has length T; targets are input shifted left by 1
Loss averaged over all T-1 positions
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def clm_step(
    token_ids: np.ndarray,
    embeddings: np.ndarray,
    W_proj: np.ndarray,
    b_proj: np.ndarray
) -> float:
    """One Causal LM training step.

    Args:
        token_ids: Token sequence, shape (T,)
        embeddings: Embedding matrix, shape (vocab_size, embed_dim)
        W_proj: Projection weight, shape (embed_dim, vocab_size)
        b_proj: Projection bias, shape (vocab_size,)

    Returns:
        Mean cross-entropy loss
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def clm_step(
    token_ids: np.ndarray,
    embeddings: np.ndarray,
    W_proj: np.ndarray,
    b_proj: np.ndarray
) -> float:
    token_ids = np.asarray(token_ids, dtype=int)
    T = len(token_ids)
    # Input: tokens[0..T-2], Targets: tokens[1..T-1]
    input_ids = token_ids[:-1]  # (T-1,)
    targets = token_ids[1:]     # (T-1,)
    # Embedding lookup
    x = embeddings[input_ids]   # (T-1, embed_dim)
    # Linear projection to logits
    logits = x @ W_proj + b_proj  # (T-1, vocab_size)
    # Cross-entropy
    n = len(targets)
    m = logits.max(axis=1, keepdims=True)
    log_sum_exp = np.log(np.exp(logits - m).sum(axis=1)) + m[:, 0]
    target_logits = logits[np.arange(n), targets]
    loss = float(np.mean(log_sum_exp - target_logits))
    return loss
explanation: Causal LM (next-token prediction) is the pre-training objective for GPT models. Each position predicts the next token using only previous context (enforced by causal masking in attention, not shown in this simplified version). The loss at each position is cross-entropy of the true next token. Averaging over all positions trains the model to predict coherently at every step. Perplexity is exp(CLM loss).

test_cases.name: Returns positive float
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
token_ids = np.array([1,2,3,4,5])
emb = np.random.randn(10, 4)
W = np.random.randn(4, 10)
b = np.zeros(10)
loss = clm_step(token_ids, emb, W, b)
assert isinstance(loss, float) and loss > 0

test_cases.name: Loss is finite
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(1)
token_ids = np.arange(8)
emb = np.random.randn(12, 6)
W = np.random.randn(6, 12)
b = np.zeros(12)
assert np.isfinite(clm_step(token_ids, emb, W, b))

test_cases.name: Perfect prediction gives near-0 loss
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
# With large logit at correct token, loss approaches 0
token_ids = np.array([0, 1])
V = 5
emb = np.eye(V, V)[:V]  # one-hot embeddings
# W maps embedding 0 to logit 100 at position 1
W = np.zeros((V, V))
W[0, 1] = 100.0
b = np.zeros(V)
loss = clm_step(token_ids, emb, W, b)
assert loss < 0.01

test_cases.name: Sequence length T=2 works
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(2)
token_ids = np.array([0, 1])
emb = np.random.randn(5, 3)
W = np.random.randn(3, 5)
b = np.zeros(5)
loss = clm_step(token_ids, emb, W, b)
assert np.isfinite(loss)

hints: Input is token_ids[:-1], targets is token_ids[1:]
